_source: Berklee College of Music curriculum, Hooktheory dataset, Jazz Theory resources
_note: Long-tail music theory. Specific voicings, extensions, substitutions not in generic training.

13th.description: Ultimate jazz chord; technically 7 stacked notes

sus4.description: Replace 3rd with 4th; wants to resolve down

7b9.description: Tritone + b9; maximum tension; bebop staple

alt_chord.description: 7 with b9 OR #9 AND b5 OR #5; 'altered' = max alterations; leads strongly to I

ii_V_I.description: Most fundamental progression in jazz; 80% of jazz standards
ii_V_I.voice_leading: Root moves down 5ths; creates smooth motion

examples: Pachelbel; countless pop songs use this

jazz_rhythm_changes.description: Based on 'I Got Rhythm'; hundreds of jazz heads built on this

blues_12_bar.quick_four: IV in bar 2 adds harmonic movement
blues_12_bar.jazz_blues_substitutions: iii-VI-ii-V instead of V7 in final 4 bars

modal_jazz.so_what_voicing: Minor 11 chord; 2 perfect 4ths + major 3rd on top
modal_jazz.dorian_mode: Natural minor with raised 6th; Dm dorian over D-7 for 16+ bars
modal_jazz.examples: Miles Davis 'Kind of Blue'; John Coltrane 'A Love Supreme' (quartal harmony)

neo_soul.characteristics: maj7#11, min9, 9sus4 chords; borrowed chords; chromatic voice leading

lofi_hip_hop.characteristics: Jazzy chords (maj7, min7, 9ths); samples; vinyl crackle; slowed BPM
lofi_hip_hop.key_elements: Dusty sample + chopped drums + simple progression + lo-fi production

tritone_substitution.description: Replace V7 with chord a tritone away (b5/b2 interval)
tritone_substitution.example: G7→Db7 in key of C; both have tritone Bb-E (enharmonic to Cb-F)
tritone_substitution.use: Creates chromatic bass line when going to I

chord_substitutions.secondary_dominants: V7 of any chord; adds temporary tonicization; borrows outside key

borrowed_chords.bVII: Major chord from parallel minor; common in rock (Bb in C major)
borrowed_chords.iv_in_major: Minor 4 chord; borrowed from parallel minor; very emotional
borrowed_chords.bIII_and_bVI: Also from parallel minor; adds dark color to major key

chord_substitutions.neapolitan_chord: bII major chord (flat-2 major); used before V7; dramatic cadence

major_modes.dorian: 2nd mode; minor with raised 6; jazzy, soulful; Carlos Santana / Daft Punk
major_modes.phrygian: 3rd mode; minor with b2; Spanish, Flamenco sound
major_modes.lydian: 4th mode; major with #4; dreamy, cinematic; John Williams, Ennio Morricone
major_modes.mixolydian: 5th mode; major with b7; bluesy, rock; 'Norwegian Wood'
major_modes.locrian: 7th mode; diminished; dissonant; rare; heavy metal

melodic_minor_modes.melodic_minor: Minor with raised 6 AND 7; smooth ascending line
melodic_minor_modes.lydian_dominant: 4th mode of melodic minor; #4 + b7; 'outside' jazz sound
melodic_minor_modes.altered_scale: 7th mode of melodic minor; all notes altered; maximum outside jazz

pentatonic_scales.blue_note: Add b5 to minor pentatonic; blues scale
pentatonic_scales.use: Most common improvisation starting point; works over almost any chord
